title: Orthoraphium coreanum
taxonomy: Orthoraphium coreanum is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is native to East Asia. The classification of Orthoraphium coreanum includes its kingdom as Plantae, and phylum as Pteridophyta. It belongs to the class Polypodiopsida and order Polypodiales. Within the family Dryopteridaceae, it is placed in the subfamily Dryopteridoideae.
uses: Orthoraphium coreanum is a plant native to East Asia, particularly in China, Japan, and Korea. Research has shown that Orthoraphium coreanum has various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant's extracts have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. Further studies are needed to fully explore the potential medicinal uses of Orthoraphium coreanum.

intro: Orthoraphium coreanum, also known as Korean licorice fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, such as nausea and diarrhea, as well as provide relief from fever and cough. The bioactive constituents of Orthoraphium coreanum include glycosides, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Orthoraphium coreanum is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions to treat various health conditions, and it is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance their effects.
